comment_text.iti111:5.1: The pe in Mahāsaṅgīti here and in similar phrases below appears to be an error, as there is no abbreviation at this point.

root_text.iti111:2.1: “Sampannasīlā, bhikkhave, viharatha sampannapātimokkhā; pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvutā viharatha ācāragocarasampannā aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvino; samādāya sikkhatha sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.iti111:3.1: Sampannasīlānaṁ vo, bhikkhave, viharataṁ sampannapātimokkhānaṁ pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvutānaṁ viharataṁ ācāragocarasampannānaṁ aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvīnaṁ samādāya sikkhataṁ sikkhāpadesu, kimassa uttari karaṇīyaṁ?
root_text.iti111:4.1: Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā, byāpādo vigato hoti, thinamiddhaṁ vigataṁ hoti, uddhaccakukkuccaṁ vigataṁ hoti, vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.iti111:4.2: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti111:5.1: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.iti111:5.2: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti111:6.1: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.iti111:6.2: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti111:7.1: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.iti111:7.2: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccatī”ti.
root_text.iti111:11.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti111:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti111:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti111:2.1: “Mendicants, live by the ethical precepts and the monastic code. Live restrained in the monastic code, conducting yourselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, keep the rules you’ve undertaken.
translation_text.iti111:3.1: When you’ve done this, what more is there to do?
translation_text.iti111:4.1: Suppose a mendicant has got rid of desire and ill will while walking, and has given up dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt. Their energy is roused up and unflagging, their mindfulness is established and lucid, their body is tranquil and undisturbed, and their mind is immersed in samādhi and unified.
translation_text.iti111:4.2: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when walking.
translation_text.iti111:5.1: Suppose a mendicant has got rid of desire and ill will while standing …
translation_text.iti111:7.1: or when lying down while awake.
translation_text.iti111:7.2: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when lying down while awake.”
translation_text.iti111:8.3: a mendicant carefully bends their limbs,
translation_text.iti111:9.2: as far as the planet extends;
translation_text.iti111:9.3: they scrutinize the rise and fall
translation_text.iti111:9.4: of phenomena such as the aggregates.
translation_text.iti111:10.3: training in what leads to serenity of heart,
translation_text.iti111:10.5: they call that sort of mendicant
translation_text.iti111:11.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti111:3.1: viharataṁ → bhavataṁ (sya-all) | kimassa uttari karaṇīyaṁ → kissa bhikkhave uttariṁ karaṇīyaṁ (bj); kimassa bhikkhave uttari karaṇīyaṁ (sya-all); kiñcassa bhikkhave uttari karaṇīyaṁ (pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.iti111:4.1: abhijjhā, byāpādo vigato → abhijjhā vigatā hoti, byāpādo vigato hoti (si, sya-all, pts1ed, csp1ed) 15A4:86 | thinamiddhaṁ vigataṁ hoti, uddhaccakukkuccaṁ vigataṁ hoti, vicikicchā → thinamiddhaṁ uddhaccakukkuccaṁ vicikicchā (15A4:86) | asammuṭṭhā → appamuṭṭhā (sya-all)
